# Quantos

> Quantos is an asset inventory and checkout platform for teams that need to know
> what they have and who has it. It tracks stock with min/max auto-reorder, checks
> physical assets out to people with a live possession ledger, turns unmet demand
> into request tickets (a purchasing signal), and enforces loss tracking that can
> never be left blank. Priced per organization — not per item.

Website: https://quantos.app/
App / sign in: https://quantos.app/app

## What Quantos is

Quantos runs the "where are our things" side of an operation from a single, fast,
mobile-friendly app. It is built for warehouses, job sites, equipment rooms, IT
asset closets, schools, and field crews — anywhere stock gets consumed and tools
get borrowed. No QR codes, no barcodes, no TV display mode: just the tracking
people actually use.

## The three modules

1. Inventory Tracker — Browse, search, and paginate items by category, tag, and
   location. Add or subtract stock; every change is written to a transaction
   audit log with a REQUIRED reason (never blank). Each item has a min and max
   stock level: when quantity drops to or below the min, Quantos calculates how
   many to order to reach the max and raises a reorder alert
   (e.g. min 5, max 25, on hand 3 -> "order 22"). Photo per item. CSV/PDF export.

2. Asset Checkout — Check an asset out to a person (who, what, when, where, and
   the expected return date), then check it back in with condition notes and an
   optional return photo. The open checkouts ARE the live possession ledger —
   who has what, right now — and overdue items are flagged when the return date
   passes. Every asset and every person has a full checkout history (chain of
   custody). Supports bulk checkout and condition tracking
   (new/good/fair/poor/damaged/retired).
   - Request tickets: when an asset is unavailable, a user submits a request
     (who needs it, what, when, urgency: low/normal/high/critical). Requests
     stack up; the most-requested item is the purchasing signal, and the oldest
     open request is first in line to be notified when an asset frees up.
   - Loss & shrinkage: marking something lost/broken/stolen REQUIRES both a
     category (lost, broken, misuse, stolen, wear_and_tear, other) AND a note.
     No blank loss records — enforced in the UI and by database constraints.

3. Reports — Most checked-out items, most-requested items (with average wait
   time), loss & shrinkage (events, category breakdown, monthly trend, cost
   impact, person responsible), inventory health (below-min items with reorder
   quantities, zero stock, overdue returns, total inventory value), and checkout
   activity (checkouts/returns per period, busiest day, who checks out most).
   Auto-generated monthly report. CSV/PDF export.

## Pricing

All three modules are included on every plan; tiers differ only by caps.
14-day free trial, no card required.

- Starter — $49/mo: 200 items, 50 assets, 5 users.
- Pro — $99/mo: 1,000 items, 250 assets, 25 users.
- Enterprise — $199/mo: unlimited items, assets, and users.

## How it is built

Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS single-page app (apps/index.html) with view switching;
Netlify Functions (serverless) for billing; Supabase (PostgreSQL with row-level
security) for data and auth; Stripe for subscriptions. Multi-tenant: every row
is scoped to an account and isolated by RLS.
